protocol_definition.visibility: Answers are private before voting; voters see all anonymized proposals during voting.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after ballot aggregation; deterministic lowest-index tie break.

After analyzing all four proposals against the task requirements:

**Proposal 1** - Only performed a single search on Semantic Scholar for "retrieval augmented generation hallucination." This is insufficient to cover the three required method families (retrieval/indexing, reranking/context selection, and citation-grounded generation/verification/guardrails/evaluation). **Proposal 2** - Performed two searches on OpenAlex covering RAG reranking/context selection AND citation grounding/verification/guardrails/evaluation. This shows better coverage of the required method families. However, the searches are broad and no follow-up lookups or source selection is visible.

**Proposal 3** - Attempted to look up specific paper IDs but used an invalid parameter ("ids" instead of "titles") and the wrong source format (OpenAlex IDs in a Semantic Scholar lookup). The tool call would fail. No actual source gathering completed.

**Proposal 4** - Only searched for "retrieval augmented generation hallucination survey 2023" on Semantic Scholar. Very narrow scope, insufficient to cover all required method families.

**Proposal 2** demonstrates the best understanding of the task requirements by searching for sources covering the specific method families required (reranking/context selection AND citation grounding/verification/guardrails/evaluation). While incomplete, it shows the most appropriate direction.
